_draft: POOL (accumulate phase; see QUIZBANK_RULES.md 7-A). All questions exam:wordpart, reconciled to medical_wordparts.json verbatim, bundle:null (unbundled). Bundling deferred to Phase B. NOT the final bank.

questions.explain: -itis = inflammation. gastritis, arthritis, dermatitis.

questions.prompt: What does the root cardi/o mean?
questions.explain: cardi/o = heart. cardiology, cardiomyopathy, bradycardia.
-ectomy = excision (surgical removal). appendectomy, nephrectomy.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix hyper- mean?
questions.explain: hyper- = above or excessive. hypertension, hyperglycemia.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix hypo- mean?
questions.explain: hypo- = below or deficient. hypothermia, hypoglycemia.
-emia = a blood condition. anemia, leukemia, hyperglycemia.
-megaly = enlargement (from Greek megas, large). cardiomegaly, hepatomegaly.
-oma = tumor or mass. carcinoma, lipoma, melanoma.
-algia = pain. neuralgia, myalgia, arthralgia.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix brady- mean?
questions.explain: brady- = slow. bradycardia (slow heart rate).

questions.prompt: What does the prefix tachy- mean?
questions.explain: tachy- = fast/rapid. tachycardia, tachypnea.
cardi/o = heart; hepat/o = liver; nephr/o = kidney; gastr/o = stomach.
hepat/o = liver. hepatitis, hepatomegaly.
nephr/o = kidney. nephrology, nephrectomy.
gastr/o = stomach; enter/o = intestine; col/o = colon.

questions.prompt: Which suffix means 'surgical creation of an opening'?
questions.explain: -ostomy = a new opening (colostomy); -otomy = incision; -ectomy = removal.
arthr/o = joint; oste/o = bone; my/o = muscle; chondr/o = cartilage.
oste/o = bone. osteoporosis, osteoarthritis.
neur/o = nerve. neuralgia, neuropathy.
gastr/o (stomach) + -itis (inflammation).
cardi/o (heart) + -megaly (enlargement).
nephr/o (kidney) + -ectomy (removal).
hepat/o (liver) + -itis (inflammation).
brady- (slow) + cardi/o (heart) + -ia.
tachy- (fast) + cardi/o (heart) + -ia.
arthr/o (joint) + -itis (inflammation).
neur/o (nerve) + -algia (pain).

options: A tumor arising in the skin
questions.explain: dermat/o (skin) + -itis (inflammation).
hepat/o (liver) + -megaly (enlargement).
-osis = abnormal condition or process. cyanosis, fibrosis.
-pathy = disease. neuropathy, cardiomyopathy.
-scopy = visual examination with a scope. colonoscopy, endoscopy.
-plasty = surgical repair/reconstruction. rhinoplasty, arthroplasty.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix dys- mean?
questions.explain: dys- = difficult, painful, or abnormal. dysphagia, dyspnea.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix poly- mean?
questions.explain: poly- = many/much. polyuria, polyneuropathy.
-penia = deficiency. leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, osteopenia.
-rrhea = flow or discharge. diarrhea, rhinorrhea, otorrhea.

questions.prompt: Which root shares the same meaning as nephr/o?
questions.explain: nephr/o (Greek) and ren/o (Latin) both = kidney. nephrology vs renal.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as rhin/o?
questions.explain: rhin/o (Greek, rhinoplasty) and nas/o (Latin, nasal) both = nose.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as ot/o?
questions.explain: ot/o (Greek, otitis) and aur/o (Latin, aural) both = ear.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as ophthalm/o?
questions.explain: ophthalm/o (Greek) and ocul/o (Latin, ocular) both = eye.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as arthr/o?
questions.explain: arthr/o (Greek) and articul/o (Latin, articulation) both = joint.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as gloss/o?
questions.explain: gloss/o (Greek, glossitis) and lingu/o (Latin, lingual) both = tongue.

questions.prompt: Which suffix shares the same meaning as -algia?
questions.explain: -algia and -dynia both = pain. neuralgia, pleurodynia.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as dermat/o?
questions.explain: dermat/o (Greek, dermatology) and cutane/o (Latin, subcutaneous) both = skin.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix a- / an- mean?
questions.explain: a-/an- = without. anemia (without enough blood), apnea (without breathing).

questions.prompt: What does the prefix anti- mean?
questions.explain: anti- = against. antibiotic, antibody, antiseptic.
-ptosis = drooping or prolapse. blepharoptosis, nephroptosis.
-stenosis = narrowing. aortic stenosis, pyloric stenosis.
my/o = muscle (myalgia); myel/o = spinal cord or bone marrow; myc/o = fungus; myring/o = eardrum.
pneum/o = lung, air; bronch/o = bronchial tube; thorac/o = chest; laryng/o = voice box. Its Latin doublet pulmon/o is tested separately.
hemat/o = blood. hematology, hematoma.

questions.prompt: What does the root aden/o mean?
questions.explain: aden/o = gland. adenoma, adenitis, adenopathy.

questions.prompt: What does the root acr/o mean?
questions.explain: acr/o = extremity or tip (also height). acromegaly, acrocyanosis.

questions.prompt: What does the root chol/e mean?
questions.explain: chol/e = bile or gall. cholelithiasis, cholecystitis, cholestasis.
-plegia = paralysis. quadriplegia, hemiplegia, ophthalmoplegia.
-pnea = breathing. apnea, dyspnea, orthopnea.
-lysis = breakdown or destruction. hemolysis, thrombolysis, fibrinolysis.
-centesis = surgical puncture to withdraw fluid. amniocentesis, thoracentesis, paracentesis.
-tripsy = crushing or breaking up. lithotripsy.
crani/o = skull; cost/o = rib; cerebr/o = brain; chondr/o = cartilage.
cyst/o = bladder; pyel/o = renal pelvis; nephr/o = kidney; ureter/o = ureter.
hyster/o = uterus; oophor/o and ovari/o = ovary; colp/o = vagina.
salping/o = fallopian tube; oophor/o = ovary; metr/o = uterus; colp/o = vagina.
pyel/o = renal pelvis; cyst/o = bladder; nephr/o = kidney; ureter/o = ureter.
aden/o (gland) + -oma (tumor); a benign glandular tumor.
chol/e (bile) + cyst (sac) + -itis; inflammation of the gallbladder.

options: Surgical fixation of a displaced kidney
questions.explain: nephr/o (kidney) + -pexy (surgical fixation).

options: Needle puncture of the chest to drain fluid
questions.explain: thorac/o (chest) + -centesis (puncture to withdraw fluid).

questions.prompt: Which root shares the same meaning as adip/o?
questions.explain: adip/o (Latin) and lip/o (Greek) both = fat. adipose vs liposuction.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as mast/o?
questions.explain: mast/o (Greek, mastectomy) and mamm/o (Latin, mammogram) both = breast.

questions.prompt: Which root shares the same meaning as hyster/o?
questions.explain: hyster/o and metr/o both = uterus. hysterectomy vs endometrium.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as dactyl/o?
questions.explain: dactyl/o (Greek) and digit/o (Latin) both = finger or toe. polydactyly vs digital.
-logy = study of. cardiology, pathology, immunology.
-gram = the record or image produced. electrocardiogram, mammogram; -graphy is the process.
-therapy = treatment. chemotherapy, immunotherapy, radiotherapy.
-uria = a urine condition. hematuria, glycosuria, bacteriuria.
-emesis = vomiting. hematemesis, hyperemesis.
-stasis = stopping or controlling. hemostasis, cholestasis, homeostasis.
-plasia = formation, development, growth. hyperplasia, dysplasia, aplasia.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix erythro- mean?
questions.explain: erythro- = red. erythrocyte (red blood cell), erythema.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix leuko- mean?
questions.explain: leuko- = white. leukocyte (white blood cell), leukemia, leukopenia.
-spasm = a sudden involuntary contraction. bronchospasm, vasospasm.
laryng/o = larynx (voice box); pharyng/o = throat; trache/o = windpipe; bronch/o = bronchial tube.
pharyng/o = pharynx (throat); laryng/o = voice box; esophag/o = esophagus.
trache/o = trachea (windpipe); bronch/o = bronchial tube; laryng/o = voice box.
bronch/o = bronchial tube; trache/o = windpipe; pneum/o = lung or air; thorac/o = chest.
thorac/o = chest; cost/o = rib; stern/o = breastbone; abdomin/o = abdomen.

questions.prompt: Which root means 'spinal cord or bone marrow'?
questions.explain: myel/o = spinal cord or bone marrow; my/o = muscle; oste/o = bone; mening/o = meninges.
adren/o = adrenal gland (sits atop the kidney); nephr/o = kidney; pancreat/o = pancreas; hepat/o = liver.
arteri/o = artery; ven/o and phleb/o = vein; angi/o = blood vessel in general.
vertebr/o = vertebra (spinal column); cost/o = rib; crani/o = skull; stern/o = breastbone.
crani/o (skull) + -tomy (incision, cutting).

options: A clot that broke free and blocked a vessel
A tendency to form abnormal clots
questions.explain: thromb/o (clot) + cyt/o (cell) + -penia (deficiency); a low platelet count.
encephal/o (brain) + -itis (inflammation).

options: A white blood cell of the immune system
questions.explain: lymph/o (lymph) + aden/o (gland or node) + -pathy (disease); enlarged lymph nodes.

options: Accumulation of fluid within the skull
questions.explain: hydr/o (water, fluid) + cephal/o (head); fluid accumulation within the skull.

options: A surgical opening from the colon
questions.explain: colon/o (colon) + -scopy (visual examination).

options: Surgical repair of a blocked blood vessel
A benign tumor of blood vessels
questions.explain: angi/o (blood vessel) + -plasty (surgical repair).
necr/o (death, dead tissue) + -osis (condition); death of body tissue.
immun/o (immune) + suppression; lowering immune activity, as after a transplant.

questions.prompt: Which root shares the same meaning as encephal/o?
questions.explain: encephal/o (Greek) and cerebr/o (Latin) both = brain. encephalitis vs cerebral.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as angi/o?
questions.explain: angi/o (Greek) and vascul/o (Latin) both = blood vessel. angiogram vs vascular.

questions.prompt: Which prefix shares the same meaning as endo-?
questions.explain: endo- (Greek) and intra- (Latin) both = within, inside. endoscopy vs intravenous.

questions.prompt: Which root shares the same meaning as gluc/o?
questions.explain: gluc/o and glyc/o both = sugar or glucose. glucometer vs glycosuria.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as pulmon/o?
questions.explain: pulmon/o (Latin) and pneum/o (Greek) both = lung. pulmonary vs pneumonia.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as muscul/o?
questions.explain: muscul/o (Latin) and my/o (Greek) both = muscle. musculoskeletal vs myalgia.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as ren/o?
questions.explain: ren/o (Latin) and nephr/o (Greek) both = kidney. renal failure vs nephrology.

questions.prompt: Which prefix shares the same meaning as pre-?
questions.explain: pre- and ante- both = before. prenatal vs antepartum.
-graphy = the process of recording or imaging (mammography); -gram is the record produced (mammogram).
-sclerosis = hardening. atherosclerosis, multiple sclerosis. Compare -stenosis (narrowing).
-rrhage = excessive flow (hemorrhage). Compare -rrhea (flow/discharge), -rrhexis (rupture), -rrhaphy (suturing).
-opsy = examination of tissue. biopsy, autopsy. Compare -scopy (viewing with a scope).
-cyte = cell. leukocyte, erythrocyte, thrombocyte.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix meta- mean?
questions.explain: meta- = change, beyond, after. metastasis, metabolism, metaplasia.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix mal- mean?
questions.explain: mal- = bad, abnormal. malnutrition, malformation, malabsorption.

questions.prompt: What does the prefix auto- mean?
questions.explain: auto- = self. autoimmune, autograft, autologous.

questions.prompt: What does the root ather/o mean?
options: Fatty plaque in an artery wall
A clot within a blood vessel
questions.explain: ather/o = fatty plaque. atheroma, atherosclerosis. Compare lip/o (fat), thromb/o (clot), sclero- (hardening).
-asthenia = weakness. myasthenia gravis. Compare -plegia (paralysis), -paresis (partial paralysis).
phleb/o = vein (phlebotomy); arteri/o = artery; angi/o = blood vessel generally; lymph/o = lymph.
tympan/o = eardrum; ot/o and aur/o = ear; audi/o = hearing.
audi/o = hearing (audiometry); ot/o and aur/o = the ear itself; tympan/o = eardrum.
odont/o = tooth (orthodontics); gloss/o and lingu/o = tongue; pharyng/o = throat.

questions.prompt: Which root means 'the membrane around the fetus'?
questions.explain: amni/o = amnion; salping/o = fallopian tube; hyster/o and metr/o = uterus.
ankyl/o = stiff, bent, fused (ankylosis); arthr/o = joint; oste/o = bone; chondr/o = cartilage.
append/o = appendix; col/o = colon; enter/o = intestine; gastr/o = stomach.

questions.prompt: Which root means 'upper chamber of the heart'?
questions.explain: atri/o = atrium (upper chamber); ventricul/o = ventricle (lower chamber); sept/o = the partition between them.
sept/o = septum, partition (septoplasty). Beware: sepsis/septic come from a different Greek root meaning rot, not sept/o.
acid/o = acid (acidosis); tox/i = poison; gluc/o = glucose; hydr/o = water.

options: Pus collection around a perforated appendix
questions.explain: appendic/o (appendix) + -itis (inflammation). Compare appendectomy (removal).

options: Hardening of arteries from fatty plaque buildup
A fatty plaque deposit in an artery wall
Surgical removal of plaque from a vessel
Formation of fatty plaque in artery walls
questions.explain: ather/o (fatty plaque) + -sclerosis (hardening). Compare atheroma (the plaque itself), atherectomy (its removal).

options: An instrument that measures pain sensitivity
questions.explain: an- (without) + alges/o (pain sensitivity) + -ic; a pain-relieving drug. Compare analgesia (the absence of pain itself).

options: Withdrawal of amniotic fluid for testing
Visual examination of the amniotic sac
questions.explain: amni/o (amnion) + -centesis (surgical puncture to withdraw fluid).

options: Stiffening and immobility of a joint
Inflammatory disease fusing the spinal vertebrae
Fusion of adjacent fingers or toes
questions.explain: ankyl/o (stiff, fused) + -osis (condition); a stiff, immobile joint.
men/o (menstruation) + -arche (beginning, onset). Compare thelarche, pubarche, adrenarche.
audi/o (hearing) + -metry (measurement). Compare audiology (the study), audiogram (the graph).

options: Derived from the person's own body
Immune attack on one's own tissue
Tissue from a donor of another species
questions.explain: auto- (self); autologous blood or tissue comes from the same individual. Compare autoimmune, autopsy.

options: An agent that neutralizes stomach acid
questions.explain: acid/o (acid) + -osis (abnormal condition).

options: Clumping of cells, as in blood typing
Clumping of red blood cells specifically
An antigen that stimulates clumping antibodies
questions.explain: agglutin/o = clumping, sticking together. Compare agglutinin (the antibody), agglutinogen (the antigen).

options: A tumor arising from star-shaped nerve support cells
A star-shaped supporting cell of the nervous system
questions.explain: astr/o (star-shaped) + cyt/o (cell) + -oma (tumor); a tumor of astrocytes.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as odont/o?
questions.explain: odont/o (Greek) and dent/i (Latin) both = tooth. orthodontics vs dentist.

questions.prompt: Which root shares the same meaning as enter/o?
questions.explain: enter/o (Greek) and intestin/o (Latin) both = intestine. enteritis vs intestinal.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as tympan/o?
questions.explain: tympan/o and myring/o both = eardrum. tympanoplasty vs myringotomy.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as vesic/o?
questions.explain: vesic/o (Latin) and cyst/o (Greek) both = bladder. vesicoureteral reflux vs cystoscopy.

questions.prompt: Which prefix shares the same meaning as mega-?
questions.explain: mega- and macro- both = large. megaloblast vs macrocyte. micro- is the opposite.

questions.prompt: Which prefix means the same as mono-?
questions.explain: mono- (Greek) and uni- (Latin) both = one, single. monocyte vs unilateral.

questions.prompt: Which prefix shares the same meaning as contra-?
questions.explain: contra- (Latin) and anti- (Greek) both = against. contraindication vs antibiotic.

questions.prompt: Which root means the same as phleb/o?
questions.explain: phleb/o (Greek) and ven/o (Latin) both = vein. phlebotomy vs venous.

questions.prompt: Which prefix shares the same meaning as ambi-?
questions.explain: ambi- (Latin) and amphi- (Greek) both = both, around. ambidextrous vs amphiarthrosis.
-edema = swelling, fluid accumulation. lymphedema, papilledema, angioedema.

options: Widening or dilation of a vessel wall
Narrowing of a passage or opening
questions.explain: -eurysm = widening, dilation of a vessel wall. aneurysm. Compare -stenosis (narrowing), -sclerosis (hardening).
-phagia = swallowing, eating. dysphagia = difficulty swallowing. Do not confuse with -phasia (speech): dysphasia.

questions.prompt: What does the root phas/o mean?
questions.explain: phas/o = speech. aphasia = loss of speech. Do not confuse with -phagia (swallowing): aphagia.
-tension = pressure. hypertension, hypotension. It is from Latin tendere (to stretch), not ten/o (tendon).

questions.prompt: What does the root phylax/o mean?
questions.explain: phylax/o = guard, protect. prophylaxis, anaphylaxis.
onc/o = tumor (oncology); path/o = disease; cyt/o = cell; crin/o = secrete.

questions.prompt: Which root means 'cornea or horny tissue'?
questions.explain: kerat/o = cornea, hard horny tissue; ocul/o = eye; retin/o = retina; blephar/o = eyelid.
pancreat/o = pancreas; hepat/o = liver; splen/o = spleen; gastr/o = stomach.
mening/o = the membranes covering the brain and spinal cord; encephal/o = brain; myel/o = spinal cord; crani/o = skull.
cervic/o = neck, or the cervix of the uterus; thorac/o = chest; lumb/o = lower back; crani/o = skull.
ureter/o = the tube from kidney to bladder; urethr/o = the tube from bladder to outside; ur/o = urine; vesic/o = bladder.
urethr/o = the tube carrying urine out of the body; ureter/o = kidney to bladder; ur/o = urine; cyst/o = bladder.

questions.prompt: Which root means 'flesh or connective tissue'?
questions.explain: sarc/o = flesh, connective tissue (sarcoma); lip/o = fat; chondr/o = cartilage; oste/o = bone.
later/o = side (bilateral); medi/o = middle; thorac/o = chest; abdomin/o = abdomen.

options: A substance capable of causing cancer
A malignant tumor of epithelial tissue
The process by which cells become cancerous
questions.explain: carcin/o (cancer) + -gen (producing); a cancer-causing agent. Compare carcinoma (the tumor), carcinogenesis (the process).

options: An agent that inhibits blood clot formation
The process of blood clot formation
A disorder of the clotting mechanism
questions.explain: anti- (against) + coagul/o (clotting); a drug that prevents clots. Compare coagulopathy (a clotting disorder).

options: Inflammation of the membranes around the brain
A benign tumor of the meninges
Protrusion of meninges through a bony defect
questions.explain: mening/o (meninges) + -itis (inflammation). Compare meningioma (tumor), meningocele (protrusion).

options: Pertaining to an upright standing position
questions.explain: orth/o (straight, upright) + -pnea (breathing); breathlessness lying flat, relieved by sitting up.

options: Preventive treatment to guard against disease
A rapidly diminishing response to repeated drug doses
The study of drug action in the body
questions.explain: pro- (before) + phylax/o (guard, protect); preventive treatment. Compare anaphylaxis, tachyphylaxis.

options: A recording of the brain's electrical activity
A recording of the heart's electrical activity
A recording of muscle electrical activity
A mineral ion that conducts electrical impulses
questions.explain: electr/o (electricity) + encephal/o (brain) + -gram (record). Compare electrocardiogram (heart), electromyogram (muscle).

options: Presence of toxins in the bloodstream
The study of poisons and their effects
Extreme dilation of the colon complicating colitis
A parasitic infection dangerous in pregnancy
questions.explain: tox/i (poison) + -emia (blood condition); toxins in the blood.

options: A severe mental disorder with loss of contact with reality
Treatment of mental disorders by psychological means
Use of medication to treat psychiatric disorders
questions.explain: psych/o (mind) + -osis (abnormal condition). Compare psychiatry, psychotherapy, psychopharmacology.

options: Presence of bacteria in the bloodstream
Presence of bacteria in the urine
An agent that inhibits bacterial growth
questions.explain: bacter/i (bacteria) + -emia (blood condition). Compare bacteriuria (bacteria in urine).

options: An agent that thins or dissolves mucus
The moist membrane lining body cavities
questions.explain: muc/o (mucus) + -lytic (breaking down); a drug that loosens mucus. Compare mucosa, mucositis.

options: Overgrowth of the horny outer layer of skin
The tough protein forming skin and nails
questions.explain: kerat/o (cornea) + -itis (inflammation). Compare keratosis (skin overgrowth), keratin (the protein).
